hero.hero_overview: Kalea, the "Surging Wave," is a Mobile Legends: Bang Bang Support/Fighter hero specializing in close-combat, high-sustain, and crowd control. She manipulates "Water Zones" to disrupt enemies, heal allies, and enhance her basic attacks, making her a versatile, mobile roamer or EXP laner with strong, reliable single-target CC.
hero.hero_strength: her strength lies in her ability to manipulate water to create "Water Zones" that empower her attacks and heal herself and her teammates.
hero.hero_weakness: Kalea's primary weaknesses in Mobile Legends include vulnerability to heavy Crowd Control (CC), which stops her conditional healing, and a reliance on proper Water Zone positioning, making her susceptible to high-mobility/burst heroes. She has a weak early game, long ultimate cooldown (~55s), and fixed, clunky attack speeds.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Role: Roam and support lanes; protect allies from early ganks.

Power Level: Low to moderate  her damage and healing are limited.

Objective:

Help secure early Scuttle Crab or Lithowanderer.

Place Water Zones for enhanced attacks and early sustain.

Set up CC for your jungler or laner.

emblems.emblem_desc: This setup boosts Kalea's early-game presence and supports her role in initiating fights and aiding teammates.

equips.equip_desc: Kalea is tanky, mobile, and supportive, making her a natural fit as a Roamer in team compositions.

hero.hero_overview: Energetic and proud, Kalea is the descendant of the Great Serpent. Initially struggling to accept her human-like form, she eventually embraces her dual heritage, using her serpent tail and fists to protect the Vonetis Sea and its inhabitants.
hero.hero_strength:
1. Enhanced Basic Attacks with Multi-Utility
Her passive empowers three consecutive enhanced basic attacks that deal damage, heal, and create new zones.

This gives her sustain, burst, and zone control in fights — especially effective in extended engagements.

2. Strong Crowd Control (CC)
Tidal Strike (Skill 2) offers knock-up effects and high mobility.

Tsunami Slam (Ultimate) provides target isolation and area knockback, ideal for picking off backline enemies.

3. Sustain and Healing
Her second enhanced basic attack heals herself and nearby allies.

This built-in healing gives her longevity in team fights without relying solely on support items.

4. Zone Creation & Repositioning Power
Her passive creates Water Zones that not only empower attacks but also allow skill synergies.

Skilled Kalea players can manipulate terrain, chain Water Zones, and outplay enemies with precise dashing and combos.

5. Flexible Role Usage
Can be played as both a Roamer (support-tank hybrid) and EXP Laner (bruiser).

She fits well in varied team compositions and can adapt based on team needs — either peeling for allies or diving the enemy backline.

6. Good Scaling and Utility
Scales well into the mid-late game.

Offers utility beyond raw damage, making her valuable even when behind — through CC, zoning, and healing.
hero.hero_weakness:
1. High Skill Ceiling
Kalea's kit requires precise timing and positioning.

Mastering the Water Zone mechanics and enhanced basic attacks can be difficult, especially for beginners.

Misusing her dash or ultimate can leave her exposed or waste crucial setup opportunities.

2. Vulnerable During Cooldowns
Her survivability and damage rely heavily on skill combos and enhanced attacks.

Once her abilities are on cooldown — especially Tidal Strike and Tsunami Slam — she becomes significantly less threatening.

3. Weaker Early Game
Limited damage and healing at early levels.

Can be bullied or zoned out by strong laners or aggressive roamers before unlocking full skill rotation.

4. Reliant on Close-Combat
Needs to be in melee range to activate enhanced basic attacks.

Prone to being kited by long-range mages or marksmen like Xavier, Lesley, or Pharsa.

5. Item-Dependent Scaling
Requires sustain or defense items (like Dominance Ice, Cursed Helmet, Oracle) to survive in longer fights.

Falls behind if starved of gold or XP, as she relies on gear to tank and sustain effectively.

6. Single-Target Ultimate
Her Tsunami Slam only targets one enemy at a time, which can be inefficient in big team fights if not aimed well.

Can be interrupted or wasted if not executed at the right time or on the right target (e.g., tank instead of marksman).

spell.spell_desc:
Activates a retaliatory shield that punishes enemies who deal damage to you.

Effect:

Activates a shield for 3 seconds.

When enemies deal damage to you during this time:

They receive 50 (+25% of damage taken) as Magic Damage back.

Your damage taken is reduced by 35% during the duration.

Cooldown: 75 seconds

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Gloo excels at disrupting enemy formations and controlling space with his Sticky Bomb (which stuns and slows enemies), and his Gloo Surge allows him to stick to enemies and draw them closer. This provides Kalea with the perfect opportunity to land her Tsunami Slam and Wavebreaker without much resistance, as Gloo can keep enemies in place, enabling Kalea to set up her skills more effectively.

counters.counter_desc: Esmeralda thrives in extended fights by stealing shields with her Stardust Dance and Falling Star, allowing her to turn defensive power into sustained damage. Since Kalea relies heavily on her Surge of Life passive for healing and durability, Esmeralda can gradually strip away her defenses and outlast her in drawn-out engagements. This makes it difficult for Kalea to benefit from her sustain, as Esmeralda continuously reduces her survivability while dealing consistent damage.

dominants.dominant_desc: To play Kalea effectively, focus on positioning yourself inside her Water Zone during fights to maximize the healing and bonus damage from her passive. Use Tidal Strike for both engaging and escaping, taking advantage of its mobility to dodge skill shots or reposition quickly. Chain Wavebreaker with Tidal Strike to set up combos and deal more damage, while also providing crowd control. When you unlock Tsunami Slam, use it strategically—wait for the right moment to catch multiple enemies or isolate a key target, and consider using Flicker to engage or escape. Flicker also helps with positioning, allowing you to close gaps or reposition in tricky situations. Build items that enhance her sustainability, like Bloodlust Axe and Oracle, so she can stay in fights longer. Pay attention to enemy positioning and use her crowd control abilities to disrupt the enemy team or peel for your own, making her a strong presence both in laning and team fights.
dominants.dominant_title: Before level 4, Kalea should focus on surviving in the lane and setting up for her ultimate ability, Tsunami Slam. During this early phase, her primary goal is to use her Wavebreaker and Tidal Strike

emblems.emblem_desc: This build focuses on Kalea's ability to sustain herself in prolonged engagements, making her a formidable presence in the EXP lane.
emblems.emblem_title: emblem set up for EXP lane kalea.

equips.equip_desc: Overall, this build focuses on Kalea's sustain, durability, and teamfight presence, allowing her to tank damage, heal herself, and stay relevant throughout extended engagements. It ensures she can handle high-pressure situations in the EXP lane, maintaining presence in skirmishes and team fights.

hero.hero_overview: Kalea is a versatile melee support-fighter hybrid who excels at diving into team fights, disrupting enemies, and sustaining allies. Drawing strength from the sea, her Water Zone mechanic empowers her basic attacks and skills, allowing her to chain combos that heal, control, and damage. She thrives in extended skirmishes and is an ideal pick for players who enjoy a high-impact, flexible playstyle.
hero.hero_strength:
Strong initiation and CC

Self and team sustain

High mobility and combo potential
hero.hero_weakness:
Requires good positioning with Water Zones

Skill-reliant, vulnerable during cooldowns

Weaker early game before full combos are available

spell.spell_desc: Provides extra mobility to reposition, chase, or escape. Especially useful to land her ultimate Tsunami Slam or retreat after diving in.
